## Building an Image Carousel in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A carousel is an index into a slides array plus a translated track. `next`/`prev` move the index (wrapping with modulo); a `translateX(-index * 100%)` slides the strip. Autoplay is an interval calling `next`, paused on hover and cleared on unmount. ### Step 1 — Index + sliding track ```vue
…
``` ### Step 2 — Prev / next with wrap ```js const next = () => { index.value = (index.value + 1) % props.slides.length; }; const prev = () => { index.value = (index.value - 1 + props.slides.length) % props.slides.length; }; ``` ### Step 3 — Dot indicators ```vue

angular.answer:
## Building an Image Carousel in Angular **Mental model:** A carousel is an index into a slides array plus a translated track. `next`/`prev` move the index (wrapping with modulo); a `translateX(-index * 100%)` slides the strip. Autoplay is an interval calling `next`, paused on hover and cleared in `ngOnDestroy`. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Index + sliding track ```typescript index = 0; @Input() slides: Slide[] = []; ``` ```html
…
``` ### Step 2 — Prev / next with wrap ```typescript next(): void { this.index = (this.index + 1) % this.slides.length; } prev(): void { this.index = (this.index - 1 + this.slides.length) % this.slides.length; } ``` ### Step 3 — Dot indicators ```html ``` ### Step 4 — Autoplay lifecycle Start in `ngOnInit`, pause via hover flag, clear in `ngOnDestroy`. ```typescript ngOnInit(): void { this.timer = setInterval(() => { if (!this.paused) { this.next(); } }, 3000); } ngOnDestroy(): void { clearInterval(this.timer); } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Single slide | Hide arrows/dots with `*ngIf`. | | Hover | Pause via a `paused` flag. | | Destroy | `ngOnDestroy` clears the interval. | **Interview tip:** Use `transform: translateX` for GPU-composited sliding, and pair `ngOnInit`/`ngOnDestroy` to start and tear down the autoplay interval — the teardown is the leak interviewers look for.
